sections.paragraph:
ἄθεος, ον (Aeschyl.+; Diog. L. 7, 119; Stoic. III 157, 606; Vett. Val. ind.; PGM 36, 319; 337; Cat. Cod. Astr. II 98, 15; 108, 3; 109, 12; Philo, Leg. All. 1, 49 al.; Just.; Tat. 6, 2 [ἄθλιος Wilamowitz]; Ath.; Iren. 1, 6, 4 [Harv. I 56, 13].—PLond 1915, 8; 27) ‘without God, godless’.

① pert. to being without a relationship to God, without God, expressed without censure (Artem. 1, 8 p. 14, 4 οὐδὲν ἔθνος ἄθεον; Maximus Tyr. 30, 2d διʼ ἀμαθίαν ἄθεοι of those who, through no fault of their own, have never heard of gods), of those who had been polytheists ἄ. ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ Eph 2:12. So also, if with Blass it is correctly restored [οὔκ] ε[ἰσὶ]ν̣ ἄ̣θ̣εοι, Ox 1 recto, 3=GTh 30; Fitzmyer Oxy 539 reconstructs ε[ἰσὶ]ν θεοί (text cited ASyn. 171, 4).

② one who disdains or denies God or the gods and their laws, god-denier, atheist (Euhemerus and other Gk. thinkers are so termed in Sext. Emp., Math. 9, 50 and 9, 17; Diogenes, Epicurus et al. in Aelian, VH 2, 31.—Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 16 Jac. p. 341, 2 οἷα ἀθέους ἐπόντωσεν; Ptolem., Apotel. 3, 14, 28 in a catalogue of vices).

ⓐ in the mouth of polytheists against Christians (also Jews: Jos., C. Ap. 2, 148) αἶρε τοὺς ἀ. away w. the atheists! MPol 3:2; 9:2a (cp. Just., A I, 6, 1; 13, 1 al.; OGI 569, 22; TMommsen, Hist. Ztschr. 64, 1890, 407; ADrachmann, Atheism in Pagan Antiquity 1922. The charge was especially volatile, since the security of the state depended on proper relationships with the gods.).

ⓑ in the mouth of Christians, w. ref. to polytheists MPol 9:2b (likew. SibOr 8, 395; Ps.-Clem., Hom. 15, 4; Clem. Al., Paed. 3, 11, 80). Of heterodox Christians ITr 10 and hence prob. also 3:2 (cp. AcPlCor 2:37).—M-M. TW.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

ind. ind. = index

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

GTh GTh = Gospel of Thomas in Coptic or in Oxy. documents—Lists 1

Fitzmyer Fitzmyer = JF.

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

ref. ref. = reference(s)

likew. likew. = likewise

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Clem. Al. Clem. Al. = Clement of Alexandria, II–III a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

prob. prob. = probable, probably

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 24
